violations.text: No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025
Observed the hand-washing sink in the kitchen lacking paper towels. Staff refilled paper towel dispenser at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025
Certified FMC 22349 has expired as of 4/22/25 license must be renewed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an food debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC place can opener in 3-bay sink to wash, rinse and sanitizer. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025
Marinara sauce, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of (PIC), (sauce) was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep to cool on 9/17/2025. Observed marinara at 46-49 degrees Fahrenheit located in walk-in cooler 9/18/2025. PIC voluntarily disposed of marinara sauce. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025
Observed the non-food contact surfaces of the (fan covers) have an accumulation of dust, and other debris located in walk-in cooler. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025

The front food prep/service area is lacking a hand-washing sink and does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. A hand sink is to be added to the front food prep/service area. Per the owner, information regarding hand sink is to be submitted by next week. Inspector contact information left with PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2023

The ceiling in the back prep area is covered with a swirled textured coating that is not smooth/durable/easily cleanable. Ceiling is to be recoated with a smooth surface and properly sealed. At the time of inspection, the owner on site stated currently working with a plumber for possible solution for hand sink. Per the PIC, information to be submitted via email by next week. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2025

The front food prep/service area is lacking a hand-washing sink and does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. A hand sink is to be added to the front food prep/service area. PIC or Owner is to contact inspector with a plan of action within 10 days from re-inspection. Inspector contact information left with PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2023

Prepared food items throughout refrigeration were observed to be lacking date marking. Food prepared on site are to be properly date marked with the date of preparation. (Per the PIC, all prepped items were recently prepared same day as inspection.) Items dated same day Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024

The deli slicer was observed to be in need of repair/adjustment. The deli slicer is missing a handle to properly adjust the machine setting. Per the PIC, a new slicer/part is on order. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024
The condensate from the walk-in cooler was observed to be draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 11/08/2024

At the time of inspection, the wall above the food slicer/prep area is open and exposing the inner wall area. Per the PIC, the opening is to be filled/sealed with a vent cover. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

The ceiling in the back prep area is covered with a swirled textured coating that is not smooth/durable/easily cleanable. Ceiling is to be recoated with a smooth surface and properly sealed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024

At the time of inspection, the establishment did not have an employee certified in food safety available/on duty at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. PIC is to ensure extra staff are certified in food safety when the certified food safety manager cannot be or is not on site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024
The hand-washing sink in the employee bathroom was lacking paper towels. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the employee bathroom. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

The front food prep/service area is lacking a hand-washing sink and does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. A hand sink is to be added to the front food prep/service area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener and strainer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Equipment was moved to the dish area for proper cleaning at the time of inspection. (If the equipment is no longer cleanable, equipment is to be discarded as needed.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

At the time of inspection, food items in the sandwich prep unit were observed held at 44 to 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per the PIC at the time of inspection, the lid to the prep unit was open a lot during lunch. Food items were moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

Prepared food items throughout refrigeration were observed to be lacking date marking. Food prepared on site are to be properly date marked with the date of preparation. (Per the PIC, all prepped items were recently prepared no longer than 7 days ago.) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

At the time of inspection, metal pieces of the scrubbing pad at the dish area were observed to be stuck to a knife in the clean knife holder. Metal scrubbing pads are to be discarded as needed when metal is frayed and/or coming off onto equipment. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

Bowls and/or portion cups were observed in containers of dry ingredients being used as scoops. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

The following equipment in the facility were observed to be in poor repair... - A strainer in the back storage area was observed to have frayed/broken wires - The deli slicer was observed to be missing a handle to properly adjust the machine setting - A knife at the clean knife holder was observed to have a melted handle which is no longer easily cleanable Equipment is to be maintained in good repair Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following equipment were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris...- Refrigeration equipment throughout (shelving, shelving coverings, gaskets, internal surfaces, external surfaces)- Storage shelving - Food storage containers- Mixer- Can Opener- Deli Slicer- Walk-In Cooler Fan ShroudsNon-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024
The condensate from the walk-in cooler was observed to be draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 11/08/2024

The walls, wall fixtures/attachments and floor in food prep and service areas behind and under equipment, as well as the walk-in cooler, were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed to be in need of repair... - The wall in the bathroom behind the toilet is open and exposing the iner wall structure- The wall above the food slicer/prep area is open and exposing the inner wall area- Shelving under the food prep area are worn The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

The front prep area does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. PIC to email inspector with proof of addition of hand sink. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2023

Bagged pasta was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023

The door to the outside in the back prep area was propped open. Outer openings of an establishment shall be protected from the entry of rodents. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023
The handle on the hand sink next to the 3-bay was not in good repair. (Handle was missing.) Handle to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023
